goal: Read program.cbl and INPUT.DAT to fully understand record layouts, FD entries, control flow, and the exact transformation logic that must be replicated.
summary: Phase 1 produced two complementary analysis documents: (1) a comprehensive 681-line spec at /app/.kimchi/ferments/019efef0-ed26-7297-91bc-5f06b16d9a8d/docs/cobol-spec.md with full PIC layouts, PROCEDURE DIVISION traces, and byte-level analysis of INPUT.DAT and the three .DAT files; (2) a focused 172-line implementation guide at /app/.kimchi/docs/cobol-analysis.md with concrete Python pseudocode, padding helpers, and edge-case checklist. Key facts established: COBOL uses 4 sequential full scans of ACCOUNTS-FILE/BOOKS-FILE per execution, INPUT.DAT is 15 bytes unterminated (pads to amount=200), .DAT files are unterminated (no LF/CR between records, exact multiples of FD record size), seed B007 is not in BOOKS.DAT so transaction will fail validation and leave all .DAT files unchanged.
stepSummaries:
1. Read /app/src/program.cbl in full and capture: SELECT clauses, FD record layouts (PIC clauses), line/record sizes, and OPEN modes. [verified]
  2. Read /app/src/INPUT.DAT and capture exact byte length of each line and field positions (hex dump if needed). [verified]
  3. Inspect the three .DAT file formats (existing snapshot in /app/data) to confirm record length matches COBOL FD. [verified]
  4. Write a Markdown spec at /app/.kimchi/docs/cobol-analysis.md summarizing the per-record transformation logic with concrete PIC mappings and pseudocode so a Builder can implement Python logic without re-reading the COBOL. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step verifications were proxies/sentinels (file existence and byte dumps), which is acceptable for an analysis-only phase — no semantic code was written in this phase.
gateVerdicts.evidence: All 4 steps verified with file existence / wc / od output

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to understand the COBOL program and produce documentation; both deliverables (681-line spec + 172-line guide) exhaustively cover record layouts, control flow, file I/O, and transformation logic.
gateVerdicts.evidence: /app/.kimchi/docs/cobol-analysis.md (172 lines) and /app/.kimchi/ferments/019efef0-ed26-7297-91bc-5f06b16d9a8d/docs/cobol-spec.md (681 lines) both exist and cover the same logical ground at different detail levels.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing was deferred in this phase; the analysis is complete enough that Phase 2 can begin implementation directly from the docs.
gateVerdicts.evidence: cobol-analysis.md includes complete pseudocode for /app/program.py including all helper functions, file I/O, validation, and update logic.
